records.ety: [L. permeatus, p. p. of permeare to permeate; per + meare to go, pass.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Permeated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Permeating.]

defs: To pass through the pores or interstices of; to penetrate and pass through without causing rupture or displacement; -- applied especially to fluids which pass through substances of loose texture; as, water permeates sand.
To enter and spread through; to pervade; as, after the first setback, the team became permeated with pessimism.
quotes: God was conceived to be diffused throughout the whole world, to permeate and pervade all things.
